title: BreakingChange
description: Records a breaking change to a governance artifact. Every breaking change, whether to a schema, policy, prompt, workflow, or convention, produces one record conforming to this schema. This is an audit artifact used by the backward compatibility workflow to track migrations and assess consumer impact.

change_id.description: Unique identifier for this breaking change record.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the breaking change was introduced.

affected_artifact.description: The governance artifact that contains the breaking change.

path.description: File path relative to the repository root (e.g., 'governance/schemas/panel-output.schema.json').

artifact_type.description: Kind of governance artifact (e.g., schema, policy, prompt). Distinct from artifact_classification which captures the high-level three-type model.

artifact_classification.description: Which governance artifact category this belongs to.

change_type.description: Whether this is an immediate breaking change or a deprecation with a migration window. Additive changes do not produce breaking change records.

severity.description: Impact severity of the breaking change. Critical: breaks all consumers immediately. High: breaks most consumers. Medium: breaks some consumers. Low: breaks edge cases only.

version_before.description: Semantic version of the artifact before the breaking change.

version_after.description: Semantic version of the artifact after the breaking change. Must be a major version bump for breaking changes.

description.description: Human-readable description of what changed and why it breaks backward compatibility.

field_or_section.description: The specific field, section, or property that changed.

change_description.description: What changed (removed, renamed, type changed, semantics changed, etc.).

previous_value.description: The previous value, type, or behavior.

new_value.description: The new value, type, or behavior.

breaking_details.description: Itemized list of specific breaking changes within the artifact.

migration_path.description: Instructions for consumers to migrate to the new version.

automated.description: Whether the migration can be performed automatically (e.g., via a migration script).

migration_script.description: Path to a migration script, if automated migration is available.

estimated_effort.description: Estimated effort for manual migration. Trivial: search-and-replace. Small: single-file changes. Medium: multi-file changes. Large: architectural changes.

deprecation_deadline.description: For deprecations (change_type='deprecation'): the date by which consumers must migrate. Must be non-null for deprecations. Null or omitted for immediate breaking changes (change_type='breaking').

consumer.description: Identifier for the affected consumer (e.g., 'governance CI workflow', 'consuming repos via init.sh', 'panel execution engine', 'Code Manager persona').

impact.description: How this consumer is affected by the breaking change.

affected_consumers.description: List of consumers affected by this breaking change.

rollback_strategy.description: How to roll back if the breaking change causes issues.

rollback_possible.description: Whether the breaking change can be rolled back.

rollback_steps.description: Steps to roll back the change.

approval.description: Approval chain for the breaking change.

approved_by.description: Who approved the breaking change (GitHub username, 'code-manager', or 'policy-engine').

approval_date.description: When the breaking change was approved.

justification.description: Why the breaking change is necessary.

pr_number.description: PR number that introduces this breaking change.

issue_number.description: Issue number tracking this breaking change.
